title: Income Tax Return (ITR) Filing
shortDescription: File your annual Income Tax Returns electronically to report income, claim deductions, and declare tax liabilities.
eligibility: Individuals, HUFs, and businesses whose gross total income exceeds the basic exemption limit.

fees.details: Filing is free. Late filing may incur penalties up to ₹5,000 under Section 234F.

requiredDocuments: Bank Statements and Investment Proofs (80C, etc.)
stepsToApply: Visit the Income Tax e-Filing portal and log in using your PAN and password.
Navigate to 'e-File' > 'Income Tax Returns' > 'File Income Tax Return'.
Select the relevant Assessment Year, filing mode (Online), and status (Individual/HUF).
Choose the applicable ITR form (e.g., ITR-1 for salaried individuals).
Review the pre-filled data, validate your income and deduction details, calculate your tax, and submit the return.
E-verify the return immediately using Aadhaar OTP, Net Banking, or an EVC to complete the process.

title: Annual Information Statement (AIS) Viewer
shortDescription: View a comprehensive statement of your financial transactions, including TDS, TCS, mutual funds, and stock market trades.
eligibility: All PAN cardholders registered on the e-Filing portal.

fees.details: Viewing and downloading AIS is free of cost.

stepsToApply: Log into the Income Tax e-Filing portal with your credentials.
Under the 'Services' tab, select 'Annual Information Statement (AIS)'.
Click 'Proceed' to be redirected to the AIS compliance portal.
Select the relevant Financial Year and view the summarized Taxpayer Information Summary (TIS) or the detailed AIS.
If you notice any incorrect transactions attributed to your PAN, submit online feedback directly on the specific transaction to correct the record.

title: e-Verify Income Tax Return
shortDescription: Mandatory electronic verification of your submitted ITR within 30 days of filing to ensure it is processed by the department.
eligibility: Taxpayers who have successfully filed their ITR but have not yet verified it.

fees.details: Free to verify.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card linked to mobile (for OTP verification)
stepsToApply: Visit the Income Tax e-Filing portal and click on 'e-Verify Return' under the Quick Links section.
Enter your PAN, Assessment Year, Acknowledgment Number, and Mobile Number.
Alternatively, log into your account, go to 'e-File' > 'Income Tax Returns' > 'e-Verify Return'.
Select your preferred verification method: Aadhaar OTP, Net Banking, Bank Account EVC, or Demat Account EVC.
Generate the OTP/EVC, enter it in the required field, and submit to finalize your ITR processing.

title: Check Income Tax Refund Status
shortDescription: Track the real-time status of your income tax refund after your ITR has been processed.
eligibility: Taxpayers who have filed their ITR, verified it, and are eligible for a tax refund.

fees.details: Status checking is free.

stepsToApply: Visit the official NSDL/Protean Refund Tracking portal or the Income Tax e-Filing portal.
Enter your PAN and select the Assessment Year for which the refund is pending.
Enter the Captcha code and click 'Proceed'.
The screen will display the current status of your refund (e.g., Processed, Sent to Refund Banker, Credited, or Failed).
If the status shows 'Failed', log into the e-Filing portal to submit a 'Refund Reissue Request' with updated bank details.

title: ITR Rectification under Section 154
shortDescription: File a rectification request to correct apparent mistakes (like tax credit mismatch or arithmetic errors) in processed ITRs.
eligibility: Taxpayers who have received an Intimation under Section 143(1) containing an apparent mistake.

fees.details: Filing a rectification request is free.

stepsToApply: Log into the e-Filing portal using your PAN and password.
Navigate to 'Services' > 'Rectification'.
Click on 'New Request', select the Assessment Year, and click 'Continue'.
Select the Reason for Rectification (e.g., Tax Credit Mismatch, Return Data Correction).
Fill in the corrected details or upload an updated XML/JSON file if reprocessing the return data.
Submit the request and e-Verify it. You will receive a Rectification Reference Number to track the status.

department: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)
title: GST Registration (New)
shortDescription: Apply for a new Goods and Services Tax Identification Number (GSTIN) to legally supply goods or services in India.
eligibility: Businesses with an aggregate turnover exceeding ₹40 Lakhs (goods) or ₹20 Lakhs (services), or those engaging in inter-state supply.

fees.details: Government portal registration is completely free.

requiredDocuments: Address Proof of the Principal Place of Business
stepsToApply: Visit the GST portal and select 'Services' > 'Registration' > 'New Registration'.
Complete Part A of the form using your PAN, Mobile Number, and Email ID to generate a Temporary Reference Number (TRN).
Log in using the TRN and complete Part B of the form, filling in business details, promoter details, and authorized signatories.
Upload all required documents, including principal place of business proofs and bank details.
Submit the application using a Digital Signature Certificate (DSC) or Aadhaar e-Sign. You will receive an ARN (Application Reference Number) to track your GSTIN issuance.

title: GST Return Filing (GSTR-1 & GSTR-3B)
shortDescription: File mandatory monthly or quarterly GST returns to declare outward supplies and pay tax liabilities.
eligibility: All registered GST taxpayers (excluding composition dealers who file CMP-08).

fees.details: Late filing attracts a late fee of ₹50 per day (₹20 for nil returns).

stepsToApply: Log into the GST Portal using your valid credentials.
Navigate to 'Services' > 'Returns' > 'Returns Dashboard'.
Select the Financial Year and Return Filing Period.
For GSTR-1 (Outward Supplies): Prepare the data online or use the offline utility to upload B2B and B2C invoice details, then submit and file.
For GSTR-3B (Summary Return): Auto-populated data from GSTR-1 and GSTR-2B will appear. Verify tax liabilities, claim eligible Input Tax Credit (ITC), and offset liabilities.
Create a challan to pay any cash balance due, offset the tax, and file using DSC or EVC.

title: GST E-Way Bill Generation
shortDescription: Generate an electronic waybill required for the movement of goods exceeding ₹50,000 in value.
eligibility: Registered GST taxpayers and transporters moving goods exceeding the threshold limit.

fees.details: Generation of an E-Way bill is free.

requiredDocuments: Tax Invoice, Bill of Supply, or Delivery Challan
stepsToApply: Visit the E-Way Bill portal and log in with your specific E-Way Bill credentials (different from the main GST portal).
Select 'E-waybill' > 'Generate New' on the dashboard.
Select the transaction type (Outward or Inward) and document type (Invoice/Challan).
Enter the supplier and recipient details, item details (HSN code, value, tax rate), and the transporter details (Vehicle Number).
Click 'Submit' to generate a unique 12-digit E-Way Bill Number (EBN) and print the PDF for the transporter to carry.

title: GST LUT (Letter of Undertaking) Filing
shortDescription: File a Letter of Undertaking to export goods or services without paying Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST).
eligibility: Any registered taxpayer exporting goods or services, provided they have not been prosecuted for tax evasion exceeding ₹2.5 Crores.

fees.details: Free to file.

requiredDocuments: Names, addresses, and occupation of two independent witnesses
stepsToApply: Log into the GST portal with your credentials.
Navigate to 'Services' > 'User Services' > 'Furnish Letter of Undertaking (LUT)'.
Select the financial year for which the LUT is being applied.
Upload the previous LUT document (if applicable/requested) and read and accept the declaration checkboxes.
Provide the details of two independent witnesses.
Sign and file the application using DSC or EVC. The LUT ARN is generated and is valid for the entire financial year.

title: EPFO Member Passbook
shortDescription: View and download your digital EPF passbook to check monthly employer-employee contributions and interest earned.
eligibility: Salaried employees with an active Universal Account Number (UAN) registered with EPFO.

fees.details: Free service.

stepsToApply: Ensure your UAN is activated on the Unified Member Portal.
Visit the EPFO Member Passbook portal.
Log in using your UAN, password, and complete the captcha.
Select the specific Member ID (if you have worked for multiple employers) from the dropdown list.
Click on 'View Passbook' to see the detailed monthly contribution history, pension accumulations, and available balance.
Click 'Download Passbook' to save the record as a PDF file.

title: EPF Online Claim (Withdrawal & Advance)
shortDescription: Apply online for final PF withdrawal, pension withdrawal, or partial PF advance for emergencies.
eligibility: Employees with an active UAN, verified KYC (Aadhaar, PAN, Bank), and linked mobile number. (Final settlement requires 2 months of unemployment).

fees.details: No processing fee. TDS may apply if withdrawn before 5 years of service.

requiredDocuments: Scanned copy of Cheque/Passbook showing name and account number
stepsToApply: Log into the EPFO Unified Member Portal.
Verify that your KYC details (Aadhaar, PAN, Bank Account) are approved under the 'Manage' tab.
Go to 'Online Services' and select 'Claim (Form-31, 19, 10C & 10D)'.
Enter your bank account number to verify and proceed to the claim form.
Select the claim type: Form 31 (PF Advance), Form 19 (Full PF settlement), or Form 10C (Pension withdrawal).
Upload the scanned copy of your cancelled cheque or passbook, verify using Aadhaar OTP, and submit the claim. The amount is usually credited in 7-20 days.

title: EPF e-Nomination
shortDescription: File your EPF/EPS nomination digitally to ensure social security for your family members without employer intervention.
eligibility: All EPF members whose UAN is active and Aadhaar is linked.

fees.details: Free to update.

stepsToApply: Log into the EPFO Member interface using your UAN and password.
Ensure your profile picture and address are updated in the 'View' > 'Profile' section.
Navigate to 'Manage' and select 'e-Nomination'.
Click 'Yes' to update family declaration. Add family members' details (Aadhaar, Name, DOB, Photo) and save.
Select the family members to nominate for EPF, allocate the percentage share (totaling 100%), and save EPF Nomination.
Click 'e-Sign' and use Aadhaar Virtual ID (VID) and OTP to digitally sign the document and complete the nomination process.

title: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)
shortDescription: National mission for financial inclusion ensuring access to a basic savings bank account, remittance, credit, and insurance.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen aged 10 years and above who does not have a bank account.

fees.details: Zero minimum balance required. No account opening fees.

requiredDocuments: MGNREGA Job Card / Voter ID (if Aadhaar is unavailable)
stepsToApply: Download the PMJDY account opening form from the official website or visit any bank branch/Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent).
Fill out the physical application form with basic demographic details and nominee information.
Attach a self-attested copy of your Aadhaar card and a passport-size photograph.
Submit the form to the bank official. The account is opened instantly, and you will be provided a passbook.
A free RuPay Debit card will be issued, which comes with an inbuilt accident insurance cover of ₹2 lakhs (subject to card usage conditions).

title: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
shortDescription: Apply for a collateral-free micro-credit loan up to ₹10 lakhs for non-corporate, non-farm small/micro-enterprises.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for a non-farm income-generating activity (manufacturing, trading, or services).

fees.details: No processing fee for Shishu and Kishor loans. No collateral required.

stepsToApply: Determine your loan category based on requirement: Shishu (up to ₹50K), Kishor (₹50K to ₹5 Lakhs), or Tarun (₹5 Lakhs to ₹10 Lakhs).
Apply online via the Udyamimitra portal (udyamimitra.in) or visit any commercial bank, RRB, or Microfinance Institution.
On Udyamimitra, register as an entrepreneur, fill out the comprehensive loan application form, and upload your business plan and KYC documents.
Submit the digital application, which is then routed to your preferred bank.
The bank will process the application, verify your credit history, and upon approval, issue a Mudra Card (working capital debit card) and disburse the funds.

title: Stand-Up India Loan Scheme
shortDescription: Bank loans between ₹10 lakhs and ₹1 Crore to SC/ST and women entrepreneurs for setting up greenfield enterprises.
eligibility: SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years setting up a greenfield (first-time) project in manufacturing, services, or trading.

fees.details: Standard bank interest rates apply. Margin money requirement is 10-25%.

requiredDocuments: Lease deed of the business premise
stepsToApply: Visit the Stand-Up Mitra portal and click on 'Register'.
Answer the brief questionnaire to assess eligibility and create a user profile.
Fill out the loan application detailing the promoter's profile, project costs, and margin money availability.
Select the specific bank branch where you wish to apply for the loan.
Submit the application online. You can track the status on the portal as the bank reviews the project report and sanctions the term loan and working capital limit.

department: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)
title: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) Enrollment
shortDescription: Guaranteed minimum pension scheme for unorganized sector workers, ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 per month post-retirement.
eligibility: All citizens of India aged 18 to 40 years holding a bank/post office savings account. (Non-taxpayers only).

fees.details: Monthly contribution amount varies strictly based on joining age and desired pension slab.

stepsToApply: Contact the bank or post office where you maintain your savings account.
Alternatively, log into your bank's Net Banking portal and navigate to the 'Social Security Schemes' or 'Investments' tab.
Select the Atal Pension Yojana (APY) option and fill out the digital enrollment form.
Provide Aadhaar details, nominee information, marital status, and select your desired monthly pension amount (₹1000 to ₹5000).
Give consent for auto-debit of the premium amount. The monthly contribution will be automatically deducted from your account until age 60.

title: National Pension System (eNPS) Registration
shortDescription: Open an NPS account online to build a retirement corpus with exclusive tax benefits under Section 80CCD(1B).
eligibility: Any Indian citizen (resident or NRI) aged 18 to 70 years.

fees.details: Initial contribution of ₹500 + nominal CRA registration charges (approx. ₹400).

stepsToApply: Visit the eNPS portal (Protean or KFintech) and click on 'National Pension System' > 'Registration'.
Choose 'Individual Subscriber', 'Citizen of India', and register using 'Aadhaar Offline e-KYC' or 'PAN'.
Fill in demographic details, select your Pension Fund Manager (PFM), and choose your investment choice (Auto or Active).
Assign nominees and upload scanned images of your photograph, signature, and cancelled cheque.
Make the initial minimum contribution of ₹500 via Net Banking or UPI.
Upon successful payment, a PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number) is generated. E-sign the form using Aadhaar OTP to complete the process.

title: Sovereign Gold Bond (SGB) Subscription
shortDescription: Invest in government-backed gold bonds to earn fixed interest (2.5%) and capital appreciation without the risk of holding physical gold.
eligibility: Resident Indian entities including individuals, HUFs, Trusts, Universities, and Charitable Institutions.

fees.details: Purchase price is determined by the nominal value of gold; online applicants receive a ₹50/gram discount.

requiredDocuments: Demat Account details (if applying for digital allotment)
stepsToApply: Monitor the RBI notifications for the opening of the SGB subscription tranches (usually issued periodically).
Log into your Bank's Net Banking portal, Post Office account, or your stock broker's Demat trading platform.
Navigate to the 'Investments' or 'e-Services' tab and select 'Sovereign Gold Bond'.
Enter the number of grams of gold you wish to purchase (minimum 1 gram, max 4 kg for individuals).
Verify your PAN and Demat account details, and complete the payment online.
Upon closure of the issue, the bonds will be allocated to your Demat account or issued as a Holding Certificate.

title: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY)
shortDescription: Open a high-interest savings account for a girl child to fund her higher education and marriage, backed by tax exemptions.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child below the age of 10 years. Maximum two accounts per family.

fees.details: Minimum initial deposit of ₹250. Max limit ₹1.5 Lakhs per financial year.

requiredDocuments: Birth Certificate of the Girl Child
Identity and Address Proof of the Parent/Guardian (Aadhaar, PAN)
stepsToApply: Download the SSY Account Opening Form from the India Post or participating commercial bank's website.
Fill out the form with the details of the girl child and the operating guardian.
Visit the nearest post office or bank branch with the filled form and original KYC documents.
Submit the documents along with the initial deposit amount (minimum ₹250) in cash or cheque.
A passbook will be issued in the name of the girl child. You can link this account to your Net Banking to make subsequent online deposits.

title: Public Provident Fund (PPF) Account
shortDescription: Long-term, tax-free (EEE) savings scheme backed by the government, ideal for retirement planning.
eligibility: Any resident Indian individual. (HUFs and NRIs cannot open new accounts).

fees.details: Minimum deposit of ₹500 per year. Max ₹1.5 Lakhs per year.

stepsToApply: Log into your internet banking portal (e.g., SBI, HDFC, ICICI) if they offer instant online PPF opening.
Navigate to the 'PPF Account' section and select 'Open New PPF Account'.
Verify your pre-filled KYC details, PAN, and Aadhaar. Provide nominee details.
Input the initial deposit amount (minimum ₹500) to be transferred from your savings account.
Submit via OTP authentication. The PPF account is created instantly, and the account number is displayed.
(Offline): Alternatively, submit Form A along with KYC documents and initial cash deposit to any Post Office or bank branch.

title: Kisan Vikas Patra (KVP)
shortDescription: A safe savings certificate scheme provided by India Post that guarantees to double your investment over a fixed period.
eligibility: Any adult Indian citizen (singly or jointly) or a minor through a guardian.

fees.details: Minimum investment of ₹1,000 and in multiples of ₹100 thereafter. No maximum limit.

stepsToApply: Download the KVP Application Form (Form-1) from the India Post website.
Fill in the applicant details, purchase amount, and nominee information.
Visit your nearest Post Office branch and submit the form along with identity and address proofs.
Make the payment via Cash, Cheque, or Demand Draft.
Upon realization of the funds, the Post Office will issue the Kisan Vikas Patra certificate (now issued in the shape of a passbook) to you.

title: National Savings Certificate (NSC)
shortDescription: Fixed-income investment scheme you can open with any post office, offering guaranteed returns and tax benefits under 80C.
eligibility: Resident Indian individuals. (Trusts, HUFs, and NRIs are not eligible).

fees.details: Minimum deposit of ₹1,000. No maximum limit.

stepsToApply: Visit any branch of the Indian Post Office.
Ask for the NSC Application Form (Form-1) and fill in your details and nominee assignment.
Submit self-attested copies of your Aadhaar and PAN card for KYC compliance.
Pay the investment amount using cash, cheque, or electronic transfer (if available at the branch).
Collect your NSC passbook/certificate from the postmaster. (Note: Interest is compounded annually but paid at maturity after 5 years).

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
title: PM SVANidhi Loan (Street Vendors)
shortDescription: Micro-credit facility providing affordable working capital loans to street vendors to resume their livelihoods.
eligibility: Street vendors possessing a Certificate of Vending (CoV) / Identity Card issued by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).

fees.details: No processing fee. Interest subsidy of 7% p.a. is credited directly.

requiredDocuments: Aadhaar Card linked to Mobile Number
Certificate of Vending / Letter of Recommendation (LoR)
stepsToApply: Visit the PM SVANidhi portal and click on 'Apply for LOR' if you do not have a vending certificate. Otherwise, click on 'Apply for Loan'.
Authenticate your mobile number via OTP and complete the Aadhaar e-KYC process.
Fill out the loan application form with your vending details, family details, and bank account information.
Select your preferred lending bank and submit the application digitally.
The bank will process the collateral-free loan (up to ₹10,000 for the first tranche) and disburse the funds directly to your account to kickstart your business.

ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
title: Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP)
shortDescription: Credit-linked subsidy program aimed at generating employment opportunities by establishing micro-enterprises in rural and urban areas.
eligibility: Individuals above 18 years, 8th pass (for manufacturing projects > ₹10 Lakhs). SHGs and Co-operatives are also eligible.

fees.details: Project costs up to ₹50 Lakhs (Manufacturing) and ₹20 Lakhs (Service). Subsidy margin ranges from 15% to 35%.

stepsToApply: Visit the KVIC PMEGP e-portal and click on 'Application for New Unit'.
Fill out the extensive online application form with personal details, proposed project details, and the sponsoring agency (KVIC/KVIB/DIC).
Select the financing bank branch from the dropdown menu and 'Save Applicant Data'.
Upload scanned copies of your passport photo, Aadhaar, project report, and education certificates.
Submit the application. The District Task Force Committee (DTFC) will interview you, after which the application is forwarded to the bank for final loan sanction and subsidy lock-in.

title: Company Incorporation (SPICe+)
shortDescription: Integrated web form providing multiple services (Name Reservation, Incorporation, DIN, PAN, TAN, EPFO, ESIC) for registering a new company.
eligibility: Any individual(s) planning to incorporate a Private Limited, Public Limited, or One Person Company in India.

fees.details: Zero MCA fee for companies with authorized capital up to ₹15 Lakhs. Stamp duty varies by state.

requiredDocuments: Address Proof for Registered Office (Utility Bill, NOC)
stepsToApply: Log into the MCA V3 Portal and navigate to 'MCA Services' > 'Company e-Filing' > 'SPICe+'.
Fill out 'Part A' to reserve your company's name. You can submit Part A independently or together with Part B.
Proceed to 'Part B' which serves as the core incorporation application, detailing capital, directors, and subscribers.
Fill out the linked web forms (e-MoA, e-AoA, AGILE-PRO-S) to apply for PAN, TAN, GST, and bank account opening simultaneously.
Download the filled PDF forms, affix the DSCs of the directors and a certifying professional (CA/CS/CMA), and upload them back to the portal.
Pay the calculated stamp duty online. Upon verification, the Registrar of Companies (RoC) issues the Certificate of Incorporation.

title: Director KYC (DIR-3 KYC)
shortDescription: Mandatory annual KYC compliance for all individuals holding a Director Identification Number (DIN).
eligibility: Every individual who has been allotted a DIN on or before 31st March of a financial year.

fees.details: Free if filed by the due date (usually Sep 30). Late filing incurs a penalty of ₹5000.

requiredDocuments: Valid Mobile Number and Email ID
stepsToApply: Log into the MCA Portal and navigate to 'MCA Services' > 'DIN Services' > 'DIR-3 KYC'.
If you are updating details for the first time or modifying existing details, download the e-Form DIR-3 KYC, fill it, affix your DSC and a professional's DSC, and upload it.
If there are no changes in your previously submitted KYC details, select the 'DIR-3 KYC Web' service.
Enter your DIN, which will pre-fill your mobile number and email ID.
Click 'Send OTP', enter the OTPs received on your mobile and email to verify them.
Submit the web form. Your DIN status will remain 'Active' for the financial year.

department: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC)
title: ICEGATE Customs Duty Payment
shortDescription: E-payment gateway to pay Customs duties, Cess, and IGST for imported goods cleared at Indian ports.
eligibility: Importers, Exporters, or Custom Brokers holding a valid Bill of Entry assessment.

fees.details: Duty amount depends on the assessment of imported goods. No portal fee.

stepsToApply: Visit the official ICEGATE portal and click on 'e-Payment' on the home page.
Enter your Document Type (e.g., BE for Bill of Entry), Location Code (Port Code), and IEC Number.
Click 'Submit' to display a list of all unpaid challans generated against your IEC.
Select the specific challans you wish to clear and choose your preferred payment gateway/bank.
Complete the transaction via Net Banking or NEFT/RTGS. Once successful, print the final e-receipt to secure out-of-charge clearance from customs authorities.

title: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
shortDescription: A one-year renewable life insurance scheme offering coverage of ₹2 Lakhs for death due to any reason.
eligibility: Any individual holding a savings bank account between the ages of 18 and 50 years.

fees.details: Premium of ₹436 per annum, auto-debited from the subscriber's bank account.

stepsToApply: Contact the bank or post office where you hold a savings account (you can also enroll via your bank's Net Banking app).
Request the PMJJBY consent-cum-declaration form.
Fill in your Aadhaar details, nominee information, and sign the consent for the auto-debit of ₹436.
Submit the form to the bank branch.
The premium will be deducted annually between May 25 and May 31. You will receive an SMS confirmation, securing a ₹2 Lakh life cover for the upcoming policy year (June 1 - May 31).

title: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)
shortDescription: A one-year accidental death and disability insurance scheme providing coverage of up to ₹2 Lakhs.
eligibility: Any individual holding a savings bank account between the ages of 18 and 70 years.

fees.details: Premium of ₹20 per annum, auto-debited from the subscriber's bank account.

stepsToApply: Access your bank's Net Banking or mobile banking application, or visit the branch physically.
Navigate to the 'Social Security Schemes' or 'Insurance' section and select PMSBY.
Verify your account details, input your nominee's name and relationship, and agree to the terms.
Confirm the auto-debit mandate. A premium of ₹20 will be immediately deducted.
The policy covers ₹2 Lakhs for accidental death/full disability and ₹1 Lakh for partial disability. The renewal auto-debit happens annually before May 31.

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
title: Pradhan Mantri Vaya Vandana Yojana (PMVVY)
shortDescription: A pension scheme exclusively for senior citizens managed by LIC, providing an assured return of 7.4% p.a.
eligibility: Senior citizens aged 60 years and above. No maximum age limit for entry.

fees.details: Purchase price ranges from ₹1.56 Lakhs (min) to ₹15 Lakhs (max) depending on the desired pension.

requiredDocuments: Bank Account details for pension credit
stepsToApply: Visit any branch of the Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) or log onto the official LIC portal.
Select the PMVVY scheme and decide on your investment amount (lump sum) and your preferred pension payout frequency (monthly, quarterly, half-yearly, yearly).
Fill out the application form with your demographic data, nominee details, and bank account information for ECS credit.
Submit KYC documents (Aadhaar, PAN, Age Proof) along with a cheque/draft for the lump-sum purchase price.
Upon realization, LIC will issue a policy document. You will receive guaranteed pension payouts for 10 years, after which the principal purchase price is refunded.

department: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)
title: NPS Tier-II Account Activation
shortDescription: Activate a voluntary, highly liquid investment account connected to your primary NPS Tier-I account.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen holding an active NPS Tier-I PRAN (Permanent Retirement Account Number).

fees.details: Minimum initial contribution of ₹1000. No minimum annual contribution requirement.

stepsToApply: Visit the eNPS portal (Protean or KFintech) and select 'Tier II Activation'.
Enter your PRAN, Date of Birth, and PAN to authenticate using the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Verify your pre-filled demographic and bank details fetched from your Tier-I account.
Choose your Pension Fund Manager (PFM) and select your asset allocation preference (Equity, Corporate Debt, Govt Bonds).
Make the initial investment payment of ₹1000 via Net Banking or UPI.
e-Sign the application using Aadhaar OTP to instantly activate your Tier-II trading account.

title: RBI Retail Direct Scheme
shortDescription: Open a Retail Direct Gilt (RDG) Account to invest directly in Government Securities, Treasury Bills, and Sovereign Gold Bonds.
eligibility: Retail investors holding a Rupee savings bank account in India, a PAN, and a valid email ID/mobile number.

fees.details: No fee for opening and maintaining the RDG account.

requiredDocuments: Bank Account details with active Net Banking/UPI
stepsToApply: Visit the RBI Retail Direct portal and click on 'Register / Open RDG Account'.
Enter your name, PAN, mobile number, and email ID to verify via OTPs.
Fill out the detailed application form, providing your bank account details for linking.
Complete the Video KYC process or Aadhaar-based offline e-KYC to verify your identity.
Upload your scanned signature and submit the application.
Once approved by the RBI, you will receive your RDG Account credentials via email, allowing you to bid in primary G-Sec auctions or trade in the secondary NDS-OM market.

title: Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM)
shortDescription: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized workers ensuring a minimum assured pension of ₹3,000/month after 60 years of age.
eligibility: Unorganized workers (street vendors, domestic workers, etc.) aged 18 to 40 years with a monthly income below ₹15,000.

fees.details: Monthly contribution ranges from ₹55 to ₹200 based on entry age, with an equal matching contribution from the Government.

requiredDocuments: Savings Bank Account / Jan Dhan Account with IFSC
stepsToApply: Visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or log onto the Maandhan portal for self-enrollment.
If self-enrolling, click on 'Click Here to Apply Now' and log in using your mobile number and OTP.
Enter your Aadhaar number, demographic details, and bank account information to generate the mandate.
The system will automatically calculate your monthly contribution amount based on your current age.
Provide consent for the auto-debit facility from your bank account.
Print the auto-debit mandate form, sign it, upload it back to the portal, and download your PM-SYM Pension Card.

title: Income Tax e-Pay Tax (Advance Tax / Self-Assessment)
shortDescription: Pay your Advance Tax, Self-Assessment Tax, or TDS online seamlessly using the e-Pay Tax functionality.
eligibility: Any individual, HUF, or corporate entity liable to pay tax to the Government of India.

fees.details: Payment depends on the taxpayer's computed tax liability. No portal fees.

stepsToApply: Visit the Income Tax e-Filing portal and click on 'e-Pay Tax' under the Quick Links section (no login required for basic payments).
Enter your PAN/TAN, re-enter it for confirmation, and provide a mobile number to receive an OTP.
After OTP verification, select the appropriate tax payment tile: 'Income Tax', 'Equalisation Levy', or 'Fee/Other Payments'.
Select the relevant Assessment Year and Type of Payment (e.g., Advance Tax (100) or Self-Assessment Tax (300)).
Enter the tax breakdown amounts (Tax, Surcharge, Cess, Interest) and click Continue.
Select your payment method (Net Banking, Debit Card, Pay at Bank Counter, RTGS/NEFT, or Payment Gateway) and complete the transaction to download the Challan Receipt (CRN).

ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
title: MSME Samadhaan (Delayed Payment Portal)
shortDescription: File online applications regarding delayed payments from buyers/government departments to micro and small enterprises.
eligibility: Any Micro or Small Enterprise possessing a valid Udyam Registration Certificate.

fees.details: Filing a grievance is completely free.

stepsToApply: Access the MSME Samadhaan portal and click on 'Case Filing for Entrepreneur / MSE Units'.
Validate your MSME entity by entering your Udyam Registration Number and mobile number to receive an OTP.
Fill in the application form detailing the buyer's name, PAN/GSTIN, and the outstanding principal amount.
Upload the PDF copies of the delayed invoices, purchase orders, and proof of delivery.
Submit the application. The system automatically sends notices to the defaulting buyer and forwards the case to the Micro and Small Enterprise Facilitation Council (MSEFC) for legal arbitration.

title: GeM (Government e-Marketplace) Seller Registration
shortDescription: Register as a seller on the GeM portal to supply goods and services directly to Government Departments and PSUs.
eligibility: OEMs (Original Equipment Manufacturers), authorized resellers, and service providers with valid business documentation.

fees.details: Registration is free. Caution money deposit (₹5,000 to ₹25,000) applies based on turnover to participate in bids.

stepsToApply: Visit the GeM portal, click on 'Sign Up', and select 'Seller/Service Provider'.
Review the terms, click 'Review & Proceed', and authenticate your organization using Aadhaar or PAN of the proprietor/director.
Enter your business details, email ID, and mobile number to create a User ID and password.
Log in and complete your vendor profile by entering GSTIN, Bank Account details (validated via penny drop), and MSME Udyam details.
Pay the required Caution Money Deposit online based on your turnover slab.
Once the profile is 100% complete, you can begin listing your product catalogs and participating in government tenders.

title: Startup India Recognition
shortDescription: Get official DPIIT recognition for your startup to avail tax holidays, self-certification, and easier public procurement norms.
eligibility: Private Limited Companies, LLPs, or Registered Partnerships existing for less than 10 years, with a turnover below ₹100 Crores, working towards innovation.

fees.details: DPIIT recognition process is completely free of cost.

stepsToApply: Register on the Startup India portal using your name, email, and mobile number.
Log into your dashboard and click on 'DPIIT Recognition' under the 'Schemes & Policies' tab.
Click on 'Apply for DPIIT Recognition' to access the detailed questionnaire.
Fill out the entity details and provide a robust write-up explaining how your startup is innovative, scalable, or solves a core problem.
Upload the Certificate of Incorporation and your business pitch deck/website link.
Submit the application. Upon successful review (usually 3-5 days), you will receive a downloadable DPIIT Recognition Certificate.

title: Post Office Monthly Income Scheme (POMIS)
shortDescription: A low-risk savings scheme that guarantees a fixed monthly income, ideal for conservative investors and retirees.
eligibility: Resident Indian individuals. Single adults, joint accounts (up to 3 adults), or minors above 10 years in their own name.

fees.details: Minimum deposit is ₹1,000. Maximum limit is ₹9 Lakhs for single accounts and ₹15 Lakhs for joint accounts.

stepsToApply: Download Form-1 (Account Opening Form) from the India Post website or collect it from the nearest branch.
Fill in the applicant details, nominee assignment, and the desired investment amount.
Visit your preferred Post Office branch with the filled form and original KYC documents (Aadhaar and PAN) for verification.
Submit the investment amount via cash (if below ₹20,000) or cheque/demand draft.
A physical passbook will be issued, and the monthly interest will be auto-credited to your linked Post Office Savings Account.

title: Mahila Samman Savings Certificate (MSSC)
shortDescription: A targeted, one-time savings scheme for women and girls offering a fixed interest rate of 7.5% for a 2-year tenure.
eligibility: Any woman for herself, or by a guardian on behalf of a minor girl child.

fees.details: Minimum deposit ₹1000. Maximum deposit limit is ₹2 Lakhs per individual.

requiredDocuments: KYC form (if not an existing customer)
stepsToApply: Download the specific MSSC Account Opening Form from the India Post website or designated commercial bank portal.
Fill out the applicant details. If opening for a minor girl, fill in the guardian's details.
Visit the Post Office or authorized bank branch with your self-attested KYC documents.
Submit the form along with the investment amount (via cheque or cash).
The official will process the deposit and hand over the Mahila Samman Savings Certificate passbook as proof of investment.

title: GST Composition Scheme Opt-in
shortDescription: Opt into the GST Composition Scheme for lesser compliance, quarterly return filing, and a fixed lower tax rate.
eligibility: Registered taxpayers with aggregate turnover up to ₹1.5 Crore (goods) or ₹50 Lakhs (services), who do not engage in inter-state supplies.

fees.details: Opting into the scheme is free of charge.

stepsToApply: Log into the GST portal using your valid username and password.
Navigate to 'Services' > 'Registration' > 'Application to Opt for Composition Levy' (Form CMP-02).
Read the 'Composition Declaration' carefully and check the affirmation boxes stating you meet the eligibility rules.
Enter the Name of the Authorized Signatory and the Place.
Submit the application by affixing your DSC or generating an EVC via mobile OTP.
You will receive an ARN indicating success, and your status will change to Composition Dealer starting the next financial year.

title: Form 15G / 15H Submission (TDS Exemption)
shortDescription: Submit a self-declaration form to your bank to prevent TDS deduction on interest income if your total income is below the taxable limit.
eligibility: Form 15G for resident Indians below 60 years. Form 15H for senior citizens (60+). Tax on estimated total income must be nil.

fees.details: Filing the form with banks or financial institutions is free.

requiredDocuments: Estimated Income details for the financial year
stepsToApply: Log into your Bank's Net Banking portal or mobile banking app.
Navigate to the 'e-Services', 'Tax', or 'Deposits' section and locate 'Submit Form 15G/15H'.
Select the Customer ID and the specific deposit accounts (FD/RD) for which you want to apply the exemption.
Fill in the estimated income for the current financial year and the total number of forms submitted previously.
Verify the details and submit using transaction password or mobile OTP.
(Offline): Alternatively, download the physical Form 15G/15H, fill it out with your PAN, sign it, and hand it to the bank manager.

title: Jan Samarth Portal (Credit-Linked Schemes)
shortDescription: A unique one-stop digital portal linking 13+ Credit Linked Government Schemes (Education, Agri, Business, Livelihood) to connect beneficiaries directly with lenders.
eligibility: Students, farmers, MSMEs, and street vendors looking for subsidized government loans.

fees.details: Portal checks and loan applications are free. Bank processing fees may apply upon sanction.

stepsToApply: Visit the Jan Samarth portal and select the loan category you need (e.g., Education Loan, Business Activity Loan).
Answer the dynamic questionnaire to check your eligibility across multiple government schemes instantly.
If eligible, register using your mobile number and OTP.
Fill out the comprehensive online application, uploading required identity, income, and business/education documents.
Review the digital offers generated from multiple partnering banks, select the best offer, and secure digital in-principle approval.

title: GST Refund Application (RFD-01)
shortDescription: Apply for a refund of excess GST paid, accumulated Input Tax Credit (ITC) due to exports, or inverted tax structures.
eligibility: Registered taxpayers who have exported goods/services without payment of tax, or possess excess cash balance in their electronic ledger.

fees.details: Free to apply.

requiredDocuments: GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B filed up to date
CA Certification (if refund amount exceeds ₹2 Lakhs)
stepsToApply: Log into the GST portal and navigate to 'Services' > 'Refunds' > 'Application for Refund'.
Select the specific reason for the refund (e.g., Refund of Excess Balance in Electronic Cash Ledger, Refund of ITC on Export).
Select the Financial Year and corresponding month/quarter.
Enter the required refund amount, which must not exceed the maximum eligible amount calculated by the system.
Upload mandatory supporting documents, export invoices (via offline utility JSON if many), and the CA certificate if applicable.
Submit Form RFD-01 using DSC/EVC. An ARN is generated to track the refund status directly to your bank account.

ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
title: CGTMSE Collateral-Free Loan Guarantee
shortDescription: Get credit facilities without collateral or third-party guarantee, backed by the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and Small Enterprises.
eligibility: New and existing Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) seeking term loans and/or working capital facilities up to ₹5 Crores.

fees.details: Annual Guarantee Fee (AGF) ranging from 0.37% to 1.35% of the guaranteed amount, paid by the borrower.

stepsToApply: Prepare a viable business plan / project report detailing technical and financial feasibility.
Approach a Member Lending Institution (MLI) such as a PSU bank, private bank, or NBFC.
Submit your loan application explicitly requesting coverage under the CGTMSE scheme.
The bank appraises your project and, upon approval, sanctions the credit facility without asking for collateral.
The bank logs into the CGTMSE portal to apply for the guarantee cover. Once the trust approves, you pay the required Guarantee Fee to activate the cover.

title: Income Tax e-Proceedings (Responding to Notices)
shortDescription: View and submit digital responses to statutory notices, intimations, and letters issued by the Income Tax Department.
eligibility: Taxpayers who have received a formal notice or intimation (e.g., Section 143(1)(a), Section 148) from the IT Department.

fees.details: Responding through the portal is free.

requiredDocuments: Supporting financial documents (Bank statements, Form 16, Capital Gains statement) proving your stance
stepsToApply: Log into the Income Tax e-Filing portal using your PAN and password.
Navigate to 'Pending Actions' and select 'e-Proceedings'.
Select the specific proceeding name/notice you wish to respond to and click 'View Notices'.
Click on 'Submit Response' next to the notice.
Select 'Agree' or 'Disagree' (fully or partially) with the notice's proposition.
Type a detailed justification in the text box, upload necessary supporting PDFs, and verify the submission using Aadhaar OTP or EVC.

title: Check PAN-Aadhaar Link Status
shortDescription: Quickly verify whether your Permanent Account Number (PAN) is successfully linked to your Aadhaar card.
eligibility: Any individual possessing a PAN and Aadhaar.

fees.details: Checking the status is completely free.

stepsToApply: Visit the Income Tax e-Filing portal homepage (no login is required).
Scroll down to the 'Quick Links' section on the left side of the page.
Click on 'Link Aadhaar Status'.
Enter your 10-digit PAN and 12-digit Aadhaar Number in the respective fields.
Click on 'View Link Aadhaar Status'. A pop-up will immediately display whether your PAN is linked to the provided Aadhaar.

title: National Savings Recurring Deposit (RD)
shortDescription: Open a 5-year Post Office Recurring Deposit account to build a guaranteed savings corpus with small monthly investments.
eligibility: Any resident Indian adult, up to 3 adults jointly, or a minor above 10 years of age in their own name.

fees.details: Minimum monthly deposit is ₹100. No maximum limit. Defaults incur a small penalty.

stepsToApply: If you have an active Post Office Savings Account with internet banking, log into the e-banking portal, select 'General Services' > 'Service Requests' > 'New Requests' > 'Open RD Account' and follow prompts.
(Offline): Download Form-1 from the India Post website or get it at the branch.
Fill in your demographic details, nominee information, and the monthly deposit amount.
Submit the form with KYC copies and the first month's deposit amount in cash or cheque.
Receive your RD passbook. Subsequent installments can be paid online via the IPPB app or Post Office Net Banking.

title: Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS)
shortDescription: A government-backed savings instrument offering high interest and regular income to individuals above 60 years of age.
eligibility: Individuals aged 60 years or above (or 55+ for retired civilian employees / 50+ for retired defense personnel under specific rules).

fees.details: Minimum deposit ₹1,000. Maximum deposit ₹30 Lakhs. Interest is paid quarterly.

stepsToApply: Download the SCSS Application form from the India Post or authorized commercial bank's website.
Fill in your personal details, age proof details, and nominate beneficiaries.
Visit the designated bank branch or Post Office with the original KYC documents.
Submit the application along with the investment amount (via cheque if above ₹1 Lakh).
Upon realization, a specific SCSS passbook is issued, and quarterly interest will automatically be credited to your linked savings account.

title: Respond to Defective Return Notice u/s 139(9)
shortDescription: Rectify and resubmit your Income Tax Return if the IT Department marks it as 'Defective' due to incomplete or inconsistent data.
eligibility: Taxpayers who have received a notice under Section 139(9) of the Income Tax Act.

fees.details: Responding to the notice is free. Must be done within 15 days of notice receipt to avoid return invalidation.

requiredDocuments: Corrected ITR JSON/XML file or supporting documents
stepsToApply: Log into the Income Tax e-Filing portal.
Go to 'Pending Actions' and select 'e-Proceedings'.
Find the notice under Section 139(9) and click on 'View Notices'.
Click 'Submit Response'. Select 'Agree' if you acknowledge the defect, or 'Disagree' if you believe the return was correct.
If you 'Agree', you must upload a fresh, corrected XML/JSON of your Income Tax Return.
If you 'Disagree', provide a detailed explanation in the text box. E-verify the response to complete the submission.

department: Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
title: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Maan-Dhan Yojana (PM-KMY)
shortDescription: A voluntary and contributory pension scheme offering ₹3000 monthly pension to Small and Marginal Farmers upon reaching 60 years.
eligibility: Small and Marginal Farmers (owning cultivable land up to 2 hectares) aged 18 to 40 years.

fees.details: Monthly contribution between ₹55 and ₹200 based on age. Can be auto-deducted from PM-KISAN benefits.

stepsToApply: Farmers can self-register via the Maandhan portal or visit the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or State Nodal Officer.
To self-register, click 'Click Here to Apply Now' on the portal and authenticate via mobile OTP.
Enter Aadhaar and Bank details. If you are a PM-KISAN beneficiary, you can opt to have the premium auto-deducted from your PM-KISAN installments.
The system will calculate the monthly contribution.
Download the auto-debit mandate form, sign it, and upload it back. The portal will generate the Kisan Pension Card instantly.
